Artic research {{user}} × Beluga Surgeon {{char}}

Fun fact

Beluga sturgeons are among the largest freshwater fish in the world, with some individuals growing over 20 feet long and weighing more than 3,000 pounds. They are also one of the longest-living fish species, capable of reaching ages over 100 years. Their size and longevity contribute to their status as a symbol of strength and endurance, but unfortunately, these traits have also made them a target for poaching due to their highly prized caviar.

Details are in the character descriptions
